g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about Link Register Offsets in ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers, essential for embedded firmware development.
g.text: Link Register Offsets in ARM Cortex-M

g.data-narration: The Link Register (R14) stores return addresses during subroutine calls and exception handling in ARM Cortex-M processors.
g.text: Stores return address when BL (Branch with Link) is executed
Holds EXC_RETURN value during exception handling

g.data-narration: Understanding LR offsets is crucial for stack unwinding, debugging, and implementing custom exception handlers in embedded systems.
EXC_RETURN values are special 32-bit patterns in the LR that encode processor state when returning from exceptions in Cortex-M.
The three most common EXC_RETURN values are 0xFFFFFFF9 for Thread/MSP, 0xFFFFFFFD for Thread/PSP, and 0xFFFFFFF1 for Handler/MSP.
In EXC_RETURN, bit 4 indicates Thread or Handler mode, bit 1 selects MSP or PSP, and bit 0 shows FP frame presence.
When an exception occurs, the processor automatically pushes an 8-register stack frame containing R0-R3, R12, LR, PC, and xPSR.
The LR is saved at offset +20 bytes from the stack pointer because it is the 6th register pushed (index 5 × 4 bytes = 20).
Cortex-M4/M7 with FPU support lazy stacking, deferring floating-point register saves to reduce interrupt latency.
g.text: EXC_RETURN bit[0]=0 indicates FP frame present

g.data-narration: In normal function calls, compilers generate prologue with PUSH {LR} and epilogue with POP {PC} to save and restore return address.
Example: Analyzing EXC_RETURN 0xFFFFFFF9 reveals Thread mode, MSP in use, and no floating-point frame needed.
g.text: Return to Thread mode using MSP, no FP state to restore

g.data-narration: With SP at 0x2000FF00, the saved LR is located at address 0x2000FF14 (SP + 20 bytes) in the stack frame.
g.text: Example 2: Calculating LR Address in Stack Frame

g.data-narration: Key formulas: LR offset = 20 bytes, common EXC_RETURN values, and bit interpretation rules for Thread/Handler and MSP/PSP.
Remember: EXC_RETURN has 0xFF upper bits, LR offset is +20 bytes, bit1=1 means PSP, and lazy stacking affects frame size.
